title: Complementary and supplementary angles (no visual)
description: Use complementary and supplementary angles to solve for a missing angle measure.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\angle a$ and $\angle b$ are complementary angles.  $\angle a$ measures $22^\circ$.  

**What is the measure of $\angle b$?**  


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\Large{^\circ}$
hints:
Here is a picture of what $\angle a$ and $\angle b$ could look like: 

[[☃ image 1]]
Complementary angles combine to create a right angle.  So, they have a sum of $90^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\angle a + \angle b &= 90^\circ
\\\\
22^\circ+\angle b &= 90^\circ
 \\\\
\angle b &= 68^\circ \end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\angle x$ and $\angle y$ are supplementary angles.  $\angle y$ measures $33^\circ$.  

**What is the measure of $\angle x$?**  


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\Large{^\circ}$
hints:
Here is a picture of what $\angle x$ and $\angle y$ could look like: 

[[☃ image 1]]
Supplementary angles combine to create a straight angle.  So, they have a sum of $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\angle x + \angle y &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\angle x + 33^\circ &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\angle x &= 147^\circ \end{align}$
